10.reason: Error: loop should be j < i to avoid printing null character

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: loop should be j < i to avoid printing null character
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: loop should be j < i, otherwise it prints the null terminator.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Loop goes one past the last character (j<=i instead of j<i), causing output of an extra null character at the end]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Loop runs one iteration too far: `j<=i` includes the null terminator or EOF character, causing an extra unwanted output (like the trailing '�'). Should be `j < i`.]

18.reason: Error: loop includes the right character, causing duplicate print when the loop continues to that character

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: loop includes the right character, causing duplicate print when the loop continues to that character
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: This prints the right character, but later the right character is printed again (since the for loop will process it and print it when it is not '-'). Need to skip printing the right character by incrementing j after the while loop.

15.reason: [Incorrect boundary checks: e.g., 's[j-1]>='0'' doesn't ensure s[j-1] <= '9'; similarly for letters. Also, accesses s[j-1] when j=0 leads to out-of-bounds read]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: conditions do not ensure that the left character is within the valid range (e.g., missing s[j-1]<='z' for lowercase). This may allow incorrect expansions.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect boundary checks: e.g., 's[j-1]>='0'' doesn't ensure s[j-1] <= '9'; similarly for letters. Also, accesses s[j-1] when j=0 leads to out-of-bounds read]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect boundary checks: e.g., `s[j-1]>='0'` does not ensure `s[j-1]<='9'`; similarly for letters. This allows mixed-type cases like 'a'-'9' to pass if `s[j-1] >= '0'` and `s[j+1] <= '9'`, even if `s[j-1]` is a letter. Also, accessing s[j-1] when j=0 leads to out-of-bounds read.]

23.reason: After expanding the range, j should be incremented by 1 to skip the end character; otherwise, the next loop iteration will process and print s[j+1] again, causing duplicate output.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  After expanding the range, j should be incremented by 1 to skip the end character; otherwise, the next loop iteration will process and print s[j+1] again, causing duplicate output.
